# Plugin Repository - AI Agent Instructions

## Overview

This repository contains a scafctl plugin project generated by the plugin template.
It may be either a provider plugin or an auth-handler plugin.

## Key Patterns

- **Plugin SDK**: Uses `github.com/oakwood-commons/scafctl-plugin-sdk`
- **Entry point**: `cmd/<plugin-name>/main.go` is the thin startup layer
- **Implementation**: Core logic belongs in `internal/...`

## MCP Preference

If this workspace has the scafctl MCP server configured in VS Code, prefer MCP
tools over shell commands for scafctl-specific tasks.

- Use MCP for provider and capability discovery: `list_providers`, `get_provider_schema`, `get_provider_output_shape`
- Use MCP for expression and template checks: `validate_expression`, `evaluate_cel`, `evaluate_go_template`
- Use MCP for solution-side debugging when testing this plugin against a scafctl solution: `inspect_solution`, `preview_resolvers`, `preview_action`, `dry_run_solution`
- Fall back to CLI commands when you need the exact user-facing command line, local build output, or behavior outside MCP coverage

## Provider Semantics

When this repository is a provider plugin, keep the provider contract coherent across all methods.

- `GetProviderDescriptor` defines the public contract: name, description, capabilities, and schema.
- `ExecuteProvider` implements that contract and should reject unknown provider names consistently.
- `DescribeWhatIf` should describe the same action without side effects.
- `ConfigureProvider` should store host configuration, not perform heavy work.
- `ExecuteProviderStream` and `ExtractDependencies` should only be customized when the provider really needs them.

Use `.github/instructions/scafctl-provider.instructions.md` for provider-specific implementation guidance.
Use `.github/prompts/provider-implementation.prompt.md` when implementing provider changes.
Use `.github/prompts/provider-review.prompt.md` when reviewing provider changes.

## Auth-Handler Semantics

When this repository is an auth-handler plugin, keep the auth lifecycle coherent across all methods.

- `GetAuthHandlers` defines the public handler contract: flows, capabilities, and display metadata.
- `Login` establishes authenticated state and should return coherent claims and expiry data.
- `GetStatus` reports auth state without causing login.
- `GetToken` should return a valid token or a clear auth error.
- `ConfigureAuthHandler` should store host configuration rather than doing heavy work.

Use `.github/instructions/scafctl-auth-handler.instructions.md` for auth-handler-specific guidance.
Use `.github/prompts/auth-handler-implementation.prompt.md` when implementing auth-handler changes.
Use `.github/prompts/auth-handler-review.prompt.md` when reviewing auth-handler changes.

## Conventions

- **Commits**: Use [conventional commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/en/v1.0.0/)
- **Signing**: All commits must be GPG/SSH signed (`-S`) and include DCO sign-off (`-s`)
- **Errors**: Return errors with `fmt.Errorf("context: %w", err)`, do not panic for recoverable failures

## Build & Test Commands

~~~bash
task build    # Build the plugin binary
task test     # Run tests
task lint     # Run linter
task lint:fix # Run linter with auto-fix
~~~

## Critical Rules

- **No hardcoded paths**: Use SDK interfaces for host interactions
- **Test coverage**: Every new file should have tests. Target 70%+ patch coverage
- **Git safety**: Never run git commit or push unless explicitly asked
